description: Cryptographic key lifecycle management including generation, distribution, storage, rotation, and destruction using HSMs, cloud KMS, and enterprise key management platforms.

layers: Cloud KMS (AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault, GCP Cloud KMS)
architecture.diagram:
[Enterprise Key Manager]
                    /          |          \
           [On-Prem HSM]  [Cloud KMS]  [Key Escrow]
              |              |              |
         [Master Keys]  [CMK/BYOK]    [Recovery Keys]
              |              |              |
         [Data Keys]    [DEK/Envelope]  [Backup Keys]
              |              |
        [Encrypted Data] [Cloud Data]

hsmOptions.notes: Strong CodeSafe (code execution in HSM) capabilities
Single-tenant HSM in AWS, supports BYOK
Fully managed HSM pool for Azure Key Vault
Backed by Cloud EKM for key management

keyLifecycle.description: Create keys using FIPS-validated RNG in HSM or trusted environment
Securely transport keys using key wrapping (KEK) or secure channels
Store keys in HSM or encrypted key store, separated from encrypted data
Access keys through authorized APIs with audit logging
Replace keys at defined crypto-periods or upon compromise
Revoke compromised or superseded keys, update CRL/OCSP
Securely destroy keys using zeroization or crypto-shredding

byok.description: Bring Your Own Key — customer generates key and imports to cloud provider KMS
benefits: Key never exists in plaintext outside customer environment
Supports compliance requirements for key custody
limitations: Key import mechanism varies by provider
Provider still uses key for encryption operations

hyok.description: Hold Your Own Key — customer retains key in own HSM, cloud requests key for each operation
benefits: Key never stored in cloud provider systems
limitations: Availability dependency on customer key service

malaysianContext.bnmRmit: BNM RMiT S10.47 requires financial institutions to maintain control over encryption keys for data stored in cloud environments. BYOK is recommended for sensitive data.
considerations: Key sovereignty — keys for Malaysian customer data should be stored within Malaysia or under Malaysian entity control
BNM expects documented key management procedures aligned with international standards
